concerns.text: FR-001 contains a raw Python traceback error ('certificate verify failed...') embedded in the requirement text. This is an implementation artifact, not a functional requirement. It should be removed or rephrased as a requirement to handle SSL verification errors gracefully, rather than stating the error itself.

FR-010 anchors to US-1 ('See US-1'), but the requirement content (collinearity diagnostics for geographic region and sampling year) directly addresses the acceptance criteria of US-3 (User Story 3: 'Given collinearity between covariates... When diagnostics are run...'). While US-1 mentions covariates, the specific diagnostic requirement is scoped to the ML validation story. Re-anchor to US-3 for clarity.
